subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: In the case of each eligible facility that is made available for the ARMS Initiative, the Secretary—

paragraph.content: shall make full use of facility use contracts, leases, and other such commercial contractual instruments as may be appropriate;

shall evaluate, on the basis of efficiency, cost, emergency mobilization requirements, and the goals and purposes of the ARMS Initiative, the procurement of services from the property manager, including maintenance, operation, modification, infrastructure, environmental restoration and remediation, and disposal of ammunition manufacturing assets, and other services; and

paragraph.chapeau: may, in carrying out paragraphs (1) and (2)—

subparagraph.content: enter into contracts, and provide for subcontracts, for terms up to 25 years, as the Secretary considers appropriate and consistent with the needs of the Department of the Army and the goals and purposes of the ARMS Initiative; and
use procedures that are authorized to be used under section 3204(a)(5) of this title when the contractor or subcontractor is a source specified in law.

paragraph.chapeau: To the extent provided in a contract entered into under this section for the use of property at an eligible facility that is accountable under the contract, the Secretary may accept consideration for such use that is, in whole or in part, in a form other than—
Forms of consideration acceptable under paragraph (1) for a use of an eligible facility or any property at an eligible facility include the following:

subparagraph.content: The improvement, maintenance, protection, repair, and restoration of the facility, the property, or any property within the boundaries of the installation where the facility is located.
The demilitarization and storage of conventional ammunition.

paragraph.content: The authority under paragraph (1) may be exercised without regard to section 3302(b) of title 31 and any other provision of law.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Pub. L. 115–232 renumbered section 4554 of this title as this section.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2021 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 116–283 effective Jan. 1, 2022, with additional provisions for delayed implementation and applicability of existing law, see section 1801(d) of Pub. L. 116–283, set out as a note preceding section 3001 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2018 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 115–232 effective Feb. 1, 2019, with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232, set out as a note preceding section 3001 of this title.

note.heading: Temporary Authority To Extend Contracts and Leases Under the Arms Initiative
note.p: Pub. L. 114–92, div. A, title III, § 343, Nov. 25, 2015, 129 Stat. 794, as amended by Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title VIII, § 809(b)(9), Aug. 13, 2018, 132 Stat. 1841; Pub. L. 117–81, div. A, title III, § 374, Dec. 27, 2021, 135 Stat. 1667, provided that: “Contracts or subcontracts entered into pursuant to section 7554(a)(3)(A) of title 10, United States Code, on or before November 25, 2025, may include an option to extend the term of the contract or subcontract for an additional 25 years.”
